title: Light-Cone Structure of Propagation of Entanglement
abstract: For a wide class of bipartite systems with localized couplings, we establish existence of an effective light-cone for propagation of entanglement. This result yields a hard lower bound on the time it takes, under ideal conditions (no loss, no decoherence), to transport entanglement to a distant location (say, a node of a graph-structured quantum network), or to maintain it there.
summary: This paper establishes a rigorous 'light-cone' for entanglement propagation in quantum many-body systems. It proves that entanglement cannot be transported between distant regions faster than a specific velocity, providing a fundamental lower bound on communication time in quantum networks.
main_problem: Establishing the existence of an effective light-cone for the propagation of entanglement in bipartite systems and determining the hard lower bounds on the time required to transport or maintain entanglement.
main_result: The paper proves that entanglement (quantified by the Schmidt number) propagates within a defined light-cone, providing an exponential bound on the leakage of entanglement outside this cone and a minimum time threshold for entanglement transport.
method: The author uses a new analysis of space-time evolution involving Duhamel's principle, trace-norm estimates in Schatten spaces, and analytic continuation of the Hamiltonian's dispersion law.
model_or_system: Bipartite systems (AB) with localized interactions, including discrete tight-binding models on lattices and continuous semi-relativistic N-body systems.
key_observables: Schmidt number (n_S), local entanglement, and trace norm distance to separability.
important_parameters: Propagation velocity (c), distance between domains (d_XY), and the error/distance from separability (kappa).
main_assumptions: The Hamiltonian must satisfy specific analyticity conditions (AH) for the dispersion law, and interactions must be localized.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of entanglement propagation, defines the mathematical framework for local entanglement and Schmidt numbers, presents theorems establishing the light-cone structure and error bounds, and concludes with technical lemmas on operator theory.
why_interesting: It provides rigorous, fundamental limits on the speed of entanglement distribution, which is critical for designing quantum networks and understanding the dynamics of information in many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
